reviews.review_text: The greatest game that never gets old or feels stale of all time. Been playing since 2006 and gonna keep playing 'til 2666.

Its like old school diablo except you travel around different mythologies, so far I've done Greece and Egypt. I killed the Scorpion King. The achievement for that is called "No More Sequels". Great game and if you like ARPGs its definitely worth it, especially on sale. Also allows dual spec builds so theres a lot of freedom when it comes to making your character. Currently running a life leech focused warrior with an axe in each hand, feels great to just be face tanking bosses but also can't wait to start a new character and do something a little more interesting. Just get it mane.

Occasional crashes and very often crashes during Ragnarok dlc, not very responsive controller input and the need to complete the game twice to unlock the last dlc (being an adult with responsibilities i became a casual gamer so that kind of gaming block just makes my life harder). Also, very rare actually useful item drop so most of the time you run with the same old skill set. Some synergies would be nice or an incentive to change the attack pattern from time to time. Rather old fashion game in the genre.
Great game really like the setting and premise one of the best arpgs!

It's a fantastic game. It's simple yet deep enough to keep you playing. I like coming back to it throughout the years, playing a different build. And every time it's loads of fun. Most of the systems and visuals still hold up pretty well even after 20 years. The new content is a hit or miss but I'm glad it's there. Recommend to everyone who dabbles in ARPGs.

Very fun game, though other people have listed similar complaints I have
-Random crashes out of the blue which can put you back some distance to the last revive point you attuned at.

-Loot tables are crazy stingy. I was on the final act of the main game before I finally found an upgrade to the boots I bought off the vendor in Athens in the First Act. So unless you're willing to look up the wiki for things you want and dedicate a hard set amount of time for a .10% drop rate, or download a community mod to boost the drop rates of uncommons and rares, you're better off making the best of what you get.

-Despite it being the anniversary edition, they 'still' haven't increased the storage capacity: making it so you 'still' have to rely on mule characters like the '06 release when your storage starts filling up, nor have they added a system to search and sort; making sifting through the heaps of relics you'll be picking up a tedious chore at times.

Looking past my complaints, I really do enjoy this game. The dual skill tree aspect makes for fun class combos. The setting is a pleasant change of pace compared to most other Diablo-likes on the market that are just dark and brooding, Titan Quest flips the script with bright and gorgeous scenery that still holds up to this day, with a lot of diverse biomes and regions, along with a lot of enemy diversity throughout the Acts that keeps the game feeling fresh.

Overall, I give the game a solid 7.5, you get a lot of content for $20 dollars between the base game and the Immortal Throne DLC that comes baked into the anniversary edition. I'd go on a limb and give an 8 considering I'm actually 'playing' more, rather than sitting in the hub looking through a wall of suffixes and affixes to determine if a ring is 1% better than the one I'm currently wearing. (Looking at you, Diablo 4..)

I've spent way too much time playing it before it was on steam

It is really fun

Diablo lite - super addictive, easy to learn but tough to master, much brighter tone than Diablo (which is exactly what I was looking for). On sale for $5 this is an easy to recommend game (worth it even at full price!)

Great game of old, works great with proton
Curious how TQ2 turns out to be

The game is old but actually quite nice and retro.
Very much like Diablo but in the sunshine (most of the time).
So why the no to purchasing it?

Well, every few seconds, as I move further into the maps, the game micro pauses (1-3 seconds).
The character is still moving in the period it pauses, so you pause and then suddenly teleport on the screen a few metres along with no control.

I have often found myself in the middle of a group of enemies, being assulted and then have to try and deal with them all at once.

Once in awhile I could deal with, but this is happening regular.

Game is old admittedly but it should run like a dream then.
Sad, I shall be deleting and continuing to look for a resolution, but don't have much hope as there are soooo many reports out there of the issue.

A money grab by the company with an issue that could be rectified by a good programmer probably in a day. In fact let claude or grok (AIs) loose on it and will likely be rectified in minutes if given the source code.
There is no UI scaling for the controller, this is a lazy way to implement the UI!! Also the controller implmentation is clunky.

It's a nice setting and colourful world. Good to have something different to the dark grim worlds of most ARPGs. But my god this a slow ARPG, even when turning up the game speed.

As you can see, I got over 7hrs in and I was still around level 10 just slowly swatting monster beasts with my electric wand. Contrast that to Grim Dawn, and in the same amount of time I'd be twice that level with multiple skills and smashing hordes of enemies in a glorious display onscreen.

The loot drops are so boring, and so far and few between that I just don't get it. Maybe ARPG gamers in the naughties were a lot more patient. I don't want a crazy fast Diablo IV type experience, but this is snails pace slow. It got very boring, which is a shame because it seemed like a nice world otherwise.

I only suggest this game if you have a lot of patience. From what I've heard it takes 10+ hours to finish act 1 alone. And if the good gear only shows up in later acts, that's a lot of grinding without much payoff.

Already buy this game with all its DLC for 3 days, it crashing on starting the game and there no response at all from the https://bugreporting.thqnordic.com/#/projects 

I think will not buy anything from thqnordic anymore

You play at your own pace !!! Game gets harder as your player gets stronger !!! There are many layers you may use to becoming a player and many ways to win !!! I use to play the game with my son's years ago but now that they have redone it I thought I would give it a try once again !!! It's fun and easy to learn for a 73 year old man !!! lol thank you TGS !!!

I've had some fun playing this game. Yet, I've left a recommendation of "No". There are many reasons for this. For frame of reference, I've got around 1300+ hours in POE, 1000+ in the Diablo series, finished Torchlight 1 & 2 multiple times, played through Grim Dawn twice, and I've forgotten what else. Here are a few of my reasons for not recommending this game (at least to friends).

Inventory is possibly the worst of all the ARPG type games I've ever played. It's a huge annoyance at the best of times. Although I did appreciate that you can purchase a small amount of extra space in your stash.

I'm in Act 4 and this game is dropping an amazing amount of white gear (what you wear for like an hour or two in Act 1). It's not even worth dropping...why is it even dropping??? I'm looking for INT mage type gear, but the game drops mostly STR gear. If I play a second playthrough, I'll have like 30-40 good blue/green pieces or gear. Annoying. Also, I would expect all bosses in Act 4 to at least drop one piece of green gear, but that rarely happens. I'll kill 7-8 bosses in a location and sometimes have absolutely nothing good drop that's even worth selling. Thankfully you make enough gold in the game to buy something half decent from merchants. This would be great for people who like spending all their time selling gear to merchants 2/3rds of their playthrough.

The fighting in this game is very finnicky and unresponsive at times. I've died a million times because my projectiles won't go where they are aiming. If I'm already shooting at a target and I try to hit another target I sometimes have to let go of all buttons on mice & keybord before aiming at new target and then press 'fire'. That's not fun.

Like me, the game feels quite dull and repetetive for long periods of time. I imaging going through all of this with another character, but it seems like that would be a chore. IDK, could be wrong.

With all that being said, if you've played all the other ARPG games like this one, go ahead and get it if it's on sale. It's the reason I got this game.

glhf

This is an awful game. 

First off, the mouse has no forgiveness in targeting either loot or monsters. I spent forever picking up items and often wound up running from the monster I was trying to target because I had clicked beyond it.

Second, the designers have no sense of balance. I went from chewing through some mobs to instantly dead, because some boss mob joined in. No warning. Just dead.

The content is neat, but the software (mouse control) and balance (instant death) issues are so frustrating and annoying that I'm sorry I wasted several hours trying to like it.
Absolute GEM of the action rpg worlds!! My actual time spent goes wayyy back before the days of steam! A must have if you love that mythology; action and kratos like smash and loot!

This is a Diablo-style action RPG with a Greek theme. While I enjoy the brighter alternate setting, the gameplay has a number of flaws that make it unenjoyable for me. One is that combat really lacks good feedback when you hit the enemy. Sometimes I would be attacking and be unsure if I was targeting the enemy or just swinging in the air. Similarly, I would sometimes be busy managing my inventory, totally unaware that an enemy crept in and started hacking away at my health! The controls feel a bit primitive. Targeting with the mouse is difficult because the screen scrolls if you click an empty space, meaning you are trying to click an empty space. Perhaps the biggest flaw I found is that the level restrictions are too severe. At level 6, I was finding level 15 equipment, which I would not be able to equip for perhaps another 10 hours of gameplay.  The difficulty scaled up after the second town, requiring me to grind, but all the equipment I was finding was way too high level and exp grinding was slowing down. At that point, I lost interest in continuing. Other flaws include a lack of healing in towns and a lack of UI scaling for high resolution displays. These issues are a shame because I really wanted to like this game.

was just ok. not really engaging plot or quests. but what causes me to give up each time a few years have passed and i think i can finally finish the game its the labyrinth in the first act. the in game made was made by the developers to confuse you to show their own superior intellect i guess. the in game map shows gaps in the maze where none exists in the isometric game and vice versa. this craptastic map alone caused me to start and abandon the game at least 5 times over the years. would never recommend it and def wont be buying anything more from this developer or named titanquest 2.
In general this is a great game, but it keeps CTD when im' in ragnarok dlc ...
Just lost several hours of gameplay because the save didnt work.

I'm truly enjoying this Diablo-like experience with real world mythology.  THre are some targeting issues but my biggest gripe, BY FAR, is weapon swapping. I use a combination of Xbox controller and Mouse/Keyboard.  Of my currtent characters 29 deaths, 28 are because I accidently clicked down on the left stick (an action I cannot NOT do durring tense moments) and swapped weapons leading to instadeath.  I may have to get 2 sets of the same weapons (or similar) and just set up the other half the same.  There's no remapping controller hotkeys.  Anyway... Enough complaining - It's a great game and unlike most other ARPG's ... you're not saddled with a super slow run/walk speed at all. In fact, you can even turn the game speed up!  That alone makes up for the minor issues I've encountered.  Get it! Got it? Good! :D

You can disable the L-click with Steam Controller mapping!  OMG it's such a relief!  I'm sure someday I may want to make a multi-weapon build but NOT THIS DAY!!!
It bring me back to the good old days!
super nice for a 10 year old game
It's a very nice Diablo 2 clone.
Yeah the game is worth it.
good old game! GOLD!
A lot of fun!
easy play once you get control figured out
Fun easy going
good game
if you liked Diablo 2, get it.
yes
